text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: for a field array whose resolver can report both a root-level error on the array itself (e.g. "needs at least N items") and nested per-index errors on individual items in the same validation pass, calling trigger() alongside a field-array action such as remove() can wipe out the nested per-index errors, leaving only (or none of) the root error behind.

Concretely: set up a form with useFieldArray where the resolver returns an errors object shaped like { test: { type: 'min', message: '...', 0: {...}, 2: {...} } } — a plain object that carries the root error's type/message directly on itself while also carrying nested errors keyed by numeric index (this is the shape useFieldArray's own internal effect leaves errors in after an array mutation). Trigger validation once — both the root error and the nested per-index errors render correctly. Now call a field-array mutator like remove(i) followed immediately by trigger('test') again. After this second validation pass, the previously-visible nested per-index error(s) disappear from formState.errors, even though the resolver is still reporting them and the indices have simply shifted after the removal.

Fix the library source so that when an existing field-array error is already a plain object carrying nested numeric-indexed errors (not a bare root-only error, and not already an array), it is not coerced/wrapped in a way that hides those nested indices — the root error info and the nested per-index errors must both remain independently readable from formState.errors after subsequent trigger() calls. Do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run the relevant tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx`. Fix the library source; do not just edit tests.

sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local

The relevant code is concentrated in `updateFieldArrayRootError`, with call sites in resolver validation and `useFieldArray`’s mutation effect. I’m reading those paths and existing regression coverage now to confirm exactly where the numeric keys are lost.

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The failure mechanism is confirmed: `convertToArrayPayload` wraps every non-array existing value, so an object like `{ type, message, 0, 2 }` becomes `[thatObject]`; the numeric item errors are then nested under index `0` instead of remaining at `test[0]` and `test[2]`. I’m checking the project’s utility conventions and type constraints before editing the shared helper.

The patch belongs in `updateFieldArrayRootError`, but the helper must distinguish three cases: existing arrays, root-only objects, and plain objects that already contain numeric item entries. I’m validating the exact state transitions and then I’ll add a focused regression test alongside the source change so the behavior is locked to the reported `remove()` plus `trigger()` sequence.

The incoming resolver result must be used as the preserved object, not the stale pre-mutation object: after `remove()`, the resolver’s numeric keys reflect shifted indices. I’ll make `updateFieldArrayRootError` retain a plain object when it already represents indexed errors, attach the root error without array-wrapping it, and add a regression test that asserts both shifted item errors and root metadata after the second trigger.

I’m refining the implementation so it also uses the current resolver object when that object contains numeric entries. That matters after removal: the current resolver result is what carries the shifted indices, while an older plain-object error must still remain readable when the new result only updates the root.

TypeScript has passed, and the build is still in progress. I found one correctness issue in the first draft: reusing the resolver’s object and then assigning `root` to it would create a self-reference. +const isIndexedObject = (

+      : isIndexedObject(existingErrors)

Implemented the field-array error preservation fix in [updateFieldArrayRootError.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts).

The helper now:
- Preserves plain objects containing numeric-indexed errors.
- Uses the latest resolver error object so shifted indices remain accurate after mutations.
- Keeps root `type`/`message` data and nested item errors independently readable.
- Avoids wrapping indexed objects in an array.
